# Second Brain Starter Template — Full Reference

## What This Is

A self-contained Obsidian vault that doubles as a GitHub repository. It implements the "LLM Wiki" pattern — a persistent, interlinked markdown knowledge base that an AI agent builds and maintains alongside a human.

The vault is agent-agnostic. It works with Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, OpenCode, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, or any agent that can read markdown and follow instructions.

## Architecture

The vault has four zones, each with distinct ownership and access rules:

| Zone | Path | Owner | Agent Access |
|------|------|-------|-------------|
| Sources | `/sources/` | Human curates, agent ingests | Read only |
| Wiki | `/wiki/` | Agent writes, human reviews | Read + write |
| Journal | `/journal/` | Human writes, agent reads | Read only |
| Schema | `/schema/` | Human sets rules, agent follows | Read only |

### Sources (`/sources/`)

Raw materials — articles, papers, transcripts, screenshots, exported data. The agent reads them but never modifies them. Each source has frontmatter tracking its processing status: `unread → reading → processed`.

### Wiki (`/wiki/`)

Synthesized knowledge — the agent's domain. The agent creates pages by reading sources, connecting ideas, and writing summaries. Pages have a status lifecycle: `draft → reviewed → stable`. Once a page is `reviewed` or `stable`, the agent cannot modify it without explicit human instruction.

A special page, `/wiki/contradictions.md`, tracks conflicting information across sources. The agent flags contradictions here instead of silently resolving them.

### Journal (`/journal/`)

The human's thinking space. Daily notes, reflections, decisions. The agent reads these for context but never modifies them. The `/daily` command creates a scaffold that the human then owns.

### Schema (`/schema/`)

The rules of the vault. Tag taxonomy, page templates, and the agent operating protocol. The agent follows these rules without exception. Only the human modifies schema files.

## Frontmatter

Every page in `/sources/` and `/wiki/` must include YAML frontmatter:

- `type`: source | wiki | journal
- `title`: descriptive title
- `created` / `updated`: YYYY-MM-DD
- `status`: lifecycle status (varies by zone)
- `sources`: (wiki only) references to source files
- `tags`: from the taxonomy in `/schema/tags.md`

## Status Lifecycle

Sources: `unread → reading → processed`
Wiki: `draft → reviewed → stable`

The agent never changes a wiki page from `reviewed` or `stable` back to `draft`. Only the human can downgrade status.

## Contradictions

When sources disagree, the agent does not silently resolve the conflict. It flags the contradiction in `/wiki/contradictions.md` with the conflicting claims, their sources, and a suggested resolution. The human decides.

## Git Workflow

- Commit after each meaningful unit of work
- Commit message format: `ingest: source-name`, `wiki: page-name`, `journal: YYYY-MM-DD`, `schema: update-tag-taxonomy`
- For solo use: commit to `main`
- For collaborative use: branch per session, merge after review
- `/sync` command or `scripts/sync.sh` handles pull-add-commit-push

## Slash Commands (Claude Code)

| Command | File | What It Does |
|---------|------|-------------|
| `/ingest` | `.claude/commands/ingest.md` | Read a source, create wiki pages, cross-reference |
| `/ask` | `.claude/commands/ask.md` | Query the wiki using existing synthesized knowledge |
| `/research` | `.claude/commands/research.md` | Research a topic, ingest sources, build wiki pages |
| `/daily` | `.claude/commands/daily.md` | Create or update today's journal entry scaffold |
| `/sync` | `.claude/commands/sync.md` | Git pull, add, commit, push |
| `/status` | `.claude/commands/status.md` | Show vault health (unprocessed sources, stale pages, contradictions) |
| `/expand` | `.claude/commands/expand.md` | Take a wiki page and deepen it from sources |

## Agent Instruction Files

| File | Agent | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `CLAUDE.md` | Claude Code | Claude-specific instructions + slash commands |
| `AGENTS.md` | Any agent | Agent-agnostic operating guide |
| `.cursorrules` | Cursor | Cursor-specific rules |
| `copilot-instructions.md` | GitHub Copilot | Copilot-specific instructions |

All four files point to `/schema/agent-protocol.md` as the single source of truth for operating rules.

## What This Is Not

- Not a plugin collection — zero plugins required, Obsidian works out of the box
- Not a sync solution — bring your own git workflow
- Not a database — pure markdown + git
- Not opinionated about which agent you use — any agent that reads markdown works

## Tag Taxonomy

Defined in `/schema/tags.md`. Four categories: Domain, Process, Status, Format. The agent uses only these tags. New tags require human approval.

## Templates

Defined in `/schema/templates.md`. Three wiki templates: Concept Page, Entity Page, Source Index. The agent copies these when creating new pages.

## Directory Structure

```
/
├── CLAUDE.md                    # Claude Code instructions
├── AGENTS.md                    # Agent-agnostic instructions
├── .cursorrules                 # Cursor rules
├── copilot-instructions.md      # Copilot instructions
├── llms.txt                     # Agent discovery (short)
├── llms-full.txt                # Agent discovery (full)
├── README.md                    # This file
├── .claude/commands/            # Claude Code slash commands
│   ├── ingest.md
│   ├── ask.md
│   ├── research.md
│   ├── daily.md
│   ├── sync.md
│   ├── status.md
│   └── expand.md
├── sources/                     # Raw materials (immutable)
│   ├── README.md
│   └── _template.md
├── wiki/                        # Synthesized knowledge
│   ├── README.md
│   ├── _template.md
│   ├── index.md
│   └── contradictions.md
├── journal/                     # Human thinking
│   ├── README.md
│   └── _template.md
├── schema/                      # Rules
│   ├── README.md
│   ├── agent-protocol.md
│   ├── tags.md
│   └── templates.md
└── scripts/                     # Utility scripts
    └── sync.sh
```
